method: Queries clustered by token-set Jaccard >=0.45 within each template. Each cluster's weighted frequency is sum across providers of (capture_count * provider_weight). Provider weights: OpenAI=1.0, OpenAI-pro=1.0, Anthropic=0.5, Gemini=0.25 — derived from BenefitsUSA real-Bing cross-check (OpenAI 58.7% match-to-Bing, Anthropic 30.8%, Gemini 38.1%). Variant labels (Equivalent/Specification/Entailment/Canonicalization/Clarification) inherited from queries-tagged-A1.json.

requiredH2Sections: Average cost without insurance (national + by metro)
Cost by sub-type (e.g., MRI by body part: brain, spine, knee, abdomen)
Hospital vs imaging center vs free-standing (with side-by-side $ table)
Self-pay / cash discount strategies
Good Faith Estimate (No Surprises Act) — how to request, sample script
Financial assistance / charity care options
Insurance options if you discover you might qualify (Marketplace / Medicaid quick check)
requiredFAQTopics: How much is an MRI without insurance in {city}?
Is a Good Faith Estimate required if I have no insurance?
What's the cheapest place to get an MRI?
Can I negotiate the cash price?
Does the price change by body part scanned?
requiredTableOrChartFormats: Side-by-side cost table: hospital vs imaging center vs free-standing (low/avg/high)
Cost by body part table (brain, spine, knee, shoulder, abdomen, MSK)
State or metro cost lookup table (top 25 metros minimum)
Self-pay vs insured cost comparison
writerAgentRecipe.stateSpecificityRule: Generate per-metro cost variants (top 50 US metros). State-level pages second priority. Bing under-fires state-only on procedures but heavily on lookup tables.
writerAgentRecipe.yearMarkerRule: Title MUST include current year. Update January each year. Cite source year for every $ figure.
writerAgentRecipe.topShapesNote: Heaviest weighting on Specification (body-part / facility-type narrowing) and Entailment (Good Faith Estimate / how-to-reduce-cost). Equivalent rephrases (cost without insurance) are table stakes.

requiredH2Sections: Average cash price (national, current month, with brand vs alternative)
Coverage by insurance type (commercial / Medicare / Medicaid by state)
Coupons + manufacturer savings programs (NovoCare for Ozempic, etc.)
Compounded vs brand: legality, price, FDA stance (current)
Alternatives for the same indication (Wegovy, Mounjaro, Zepbound, generic semaglutide)
Pharmacy price comparison (CVS, Walgreens, Costco, Mark Cuban Cost Plus, GoodRx)
Telehealth + compounded routes (Hims, Ro, Henry Meds — current pricing)
requiredFAQTopics: How much does Ozempic cost without insurance?
Does Medicare cover Ozempic for weight loss vs diabetes?
Is compounded semaglutide still legal in {year}?
What's the cheapest pharmacy for Ozempic?
Can I use a manufacturer coupon if I have Medicare?
requiredTableOrChartFormats: Pharmacy price comparison table (top 10 chains + online)
Insurance coverage matrix (commercial / Medicare Part D / Medicaid by state)
Brand vs alternative comparison ($ + indication coverage)
Coupon stacking flowchart
writerAgentRecipe.stateSpecificityRule: Medicaid coverage of GLP-1 for weight loss varies by state — generate per-state coverage tables. Pharmacy availability is national.
writerAgentRecipe.yearMarkerRule: Year-anchored title and cash price figures. Compounded legal status changes — date-stamp the compounded section explicitly.
writerAgentRecipe.topShapesNote: Heavy on Specification (by-pharmacy, by-indication) and Entailment (alternatives, coupons). The 'compounded vs brand' axis is a major sub-shape that doesn't appear in the Equivalent layer.

requiredH2Sections: Direct yes/no answer in first 50 words (with the year-current caveat)
What's actually covered vs not covered (bullet list)
Plan-type breakdown (Original Medicare vs Medicare Advantage vs standalone dental for Medicare-dental; or expansion vs non-expansion for state-Medicaid)
Cost / income limit table (current year, by household size for Medicaid)
How to apply / where to enroll
State-specific exceptions (link out to state pages)
Common scenarios / edge cases as Q&A blocks
requiredFAQTopics: Direct phrasing of the question itself ('Does Medicare cover dental?')
Year-anchored variant ('Does Medicare cover dental in {year}?')
State-anchored variant if state-Medicaid ('Does {state} Medicaid cover X?')
Eligibility-by-household-size variants for state-Medicaid
What changed this year
requiredTableOrChartFormats: Coverage matrix (procedure × plan type)
State Medicaid income limits table by household size (current year) — REQUIRED for state-medicaid Q&A pages
Plan type comparison (Original Medicare vs MA vs standalone dental)
writerAgentRecipe.stateSpecificityRule: CRITICAL — state-Medicaid Q&A queries are the #1 Bing blind spot from BenefitsUSA crosscheck (Texas, Missouri, Ohio, SC, Florida — 1000+ citations each). Generate one Q&A page per (state × question). Use canonical state Medicaid program names (Medi-Cal, MO HealthNet, SoonerCare, MainerCare, MNsure).
writerAgentRecipe.yearMarkerRule: Title MUST include year for income-limit and coverage Q&As. Re-publish each January. Year omitted = invisible to Bing.
writerAgentRecipe.topShapesNote: Equivalent (rephrase of the question) dominates raw count. But Specification (state × question, plan-type × question, household-size × question) drives Bing match because Bing decomposes geographic/program permutations heavily and our LLM proxies don't.

requiredH2Sections: One-sentence plain-English definition (above the fold)
What's included / what's NOT included (bullet list — drives the Specification queries)
Step-by-step worked example with real numbers (current year)
Edge cases (401(k) withdrawals, Roth, SSDI/SSI, self-employed deductions)
How it connects to the user action (subsidies, Medicaid eligibility)
Worksheet / calculator embed
Where to find the official IRS / HealthCare.gov form lines
requiredFAQTopics: What counts as MAGI?
Does {edge case income source} count? (401k, SSDI, gambling, Roth, capital gains)
How do I report income changes?
MAGI for Medicaid vs MAGI for Marketplace (same/different)
Self-employed MAGI calculation
requiredTableOrChartFormats: What counts vs what doesn't count comparison table
MAGI worked example (line-by-line with real numbers)
Side-by-side: AGI vs MAGI
writerAgentRecipe.stateSpecificityRule: MAGI is federal — no state variants needed. (CAUTION: Bing crosscheck shows only 4 MAGI queries in 2 months across all of BenefitsUSA. This template is over-engineered relative to actual Bing demand. Build ONE strong page, don't fan out.)
writerAgentRecipe.yearMarkerRule: Year-anchor the worked example and form line references. Definition itself is evergreen.
writerAgentRecipe.topShapesNote: Heavy Entailment (edge-case income sources — 401k is the dominant overshoot per BenefitsUSA crosscheck) and Canonicalization (MAGI = Modified AGI = …). Bing demand is THIN — prioritize page quality over fan-out.

Your Special Enrollment Period (SEP) window — exact dates, with day-by-day countdown

Documents needed to prove the qualifying event

Coverage option comparison ($ + start date + duration)
Document checklist
writerAgentRecipe.stateSpecificityRule: Generate state variants for state-based marketplaces (CA, NY, MA, MN, WA, CO, CT, MD, NJ, NV, OR, PA, RI, VT, DC, etc.). Federal Marketplace states share one page.
writerAgentRecipe.yearMarkerRule: SEP rules are year-stable but cost figures + subsidy thresholds change. Year-anchor the cost section.
writerAgentRecipe.topShapesNote: Specification heavy (event × action: 'turning 26 + COBRA', 'turning 26 + Marketplace SEP') and Entailment ('what to do next' implied by the event). Equivalent is light — the event itself is the search query, not the rephrase.

requiredH2Sections: Why coverage is hard for {persona} (pain points)
Quick eligibility check (income / household / state)
Best path #1: Marketplace + subsidies (with the 1099 income calculation worked example)
Best path #2: Medicaid (state-by-state for variable coverage)
Best path #3: Spouse or parent plan
Tax considerations specific to this persona (self-employed health insurance deduction, etc.)
Real-world cost example with their typical income range
How to estimate variable income on the application
requiredFAQTopics: How does {persona} qualify for Marketplace subsidies?
How do I report variable / 1099 income?
Can {persona} get Medicaid?
What deductions can {persona} take to lower MAGI?
What about spouse/parent coverage?
requiredTableOrChartFormats: Income → subsidy estimator table (for this persona's typical range)
State Medicaid eligibility map for {persona}
Coverage path decision tree
writerAgentRecipe.stateSpecificityRule: Generate state variants for state-marketplace states + Medicaid-expansion states. Persona × state pages will be Bing's preferred decomposition.
writerAgentRecipe.yearMarkerRule: Income thresholds and subsidy calculations year-anchored.
writerAgentRecipe.topShapesNote: Specification dominates (persona × scenario, persona × state). Entailment is heavy (implied next steps: how to apply, how to estimate income). The persona itself is canonical — don't over-generate Equivalent rephrases.

requiredH2Sections: Available {state} Medicare Advantage plans this year (with carrier list)
Plan finder lookup table (county-by-county within the state)
Top-rated plans by Star Rating (CMS data, current year)
$0 premium plans available in {state}
Key benefits compared (dental, vision, hearing, OTC, fitness, transportation)
Provider network considerations (HMO vs PPO vs PFFS in this state)
How to enroll + AEP/OEP/SEP windows
State-specific extras (some states have unique MA programs)
requiredFAQTopics: Best Medicare Advantage plans in {state} {year}?
Are there $0 premium MA plans in {county}, {state}?
Which {state} MA plans include dental?
How do I switch MA plans in {state}?
What's the AEP for {year}?
requiredTableOrChartFormats: County-by-county plan availability table (CRITICAL)
Carrier comparison ($, network, benefits, star rating)
Star rating leaderboard for the state
Benefits comparison matrix
writerAgentRecipe.stateSpecificityRule: REQUIRED state-level page per state, plus county-level lookup tables. MA shopping is hyperlocal — county-level granularity beats state-only.
writerAgentRecipe.yearMarkerRule: Plan year MUST be in title. Re-generate every September when CMS publishes new plan data. Stale year = zero traffic.
writerAgentRecipe.topShapesNote: Specification heavy (state × plan-type, state × benefit, state × carrier, county × plan). Equivalent ('best Medicare Advantage in California') is the entry point but Specification is what Bing decomposes into.

requiredH2Sections: The number / chart up top (FPL table, eligibility threshold, key figure for the day)
What it means in plain English (one paragraph)
Eligibility implications (Medicaid, CHIP, Marketplace subsidies — by household size)
State-by-state breakdown of how this number is used
Worked example for a specific household scenario
What changed this year (vs last year)
Calculator / lookup tool embed
Related programs (SNAP, WIC) that use this number
requiredFAQTopics: What is the FPL for {year}?
What is the FPL for a family of {N}?
Do I qualify for Medicaid at {income}?
How does FPL connect to subsidies (138%, 150%, 200%, 400%)?
{state} Medicaid income limits
requiredTableOrChartFormats: Federal Poverty Level table by household size (1-8+) for current year — THE primary asset
FPL % thresholds table (138%, 150%, 200%, 250%, 400% by household size)
State Medicaid expansion status map
Year-over-year FPL change table
writerAgentRecipe.stateSpecificityRule: CRITICAL — top Bing blind spot is per-state Medicaid income limit pages (Texas, Missouri, Ohio, SC, Florida, California). Generate one page per state + per program (state Medicaid, SNAP, WIC) that consumes FPL.
writerAgentRecipe.yearMarkerRule: MANDATORY year in title. Republish JAN 15 each year when ASPE publishes new guidelines. Stale year = invisible to Bing.
writerAgentRecipe.topShapesNote: Equivalent dominates raw count (FPL 2026, poverty guidelines 2026 — Bing-validated 8/8 with 875% overlap). But the BIG missed shape is Specification: state × program (Texas Medicaid, Missouri Medicaid, etc.) drives 4000+ citations/month per BenefitsUSA crosscheck. THIS is the formula upgrade.
